title: 8 Using the Account Groups Function
body_text:
8	Using the Account Groups Function
8.1	Overview of CAS Online Account Groups
Use account groups to control which accounts are displayed on the Account Activity and Account Summary pages. An account group can be set up as the default group the user sees upon login to CAS Online. Alternatively, the account group can be activated after the user logs in to CAS Online. Use of this feature is optional and is only available to users who have access to multiple accounts (Transit Numbers) or sub-accounts by currency. The userГўВҖВҷs display settings donГўВҖВҷt affect other users and can be changed at anytime.
Account groups are most useful for financial institutions that have multiple settlement accounts. For example, if a user has access to 20 accounts (Transit Numbers) but works regularly with five of them, the user can create a group that consists of just those five accounts.
If the financial institution has one settlement account, the usefulness of the account groups function is limited, but can be used to control which sub-accounts are displayed. For example, the user may want to see the Canadian dollar sub-account but not the US dollar sub-account.
8.2	Creating an Account Group
To create an account group:
Log in to CAS Online. Select
Account Groups
,
then select
Create New Account Group
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Account Groups
Complete the Create Account Group page. See Figure 2.
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the name of the new account group in the Group Name field.
ГўВҲВ’
Check the Group box beside each account that will be included in the group.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Continue
.
Figure 2: Create Account Group
Select
Confirm
when prompted.
8.3	Managing Account Group Settings
There are two account group settings. The user can designate which account group appears at login to CAS Online and which account group is active. The account group that is designated as active is the group the user has access to while using CAS Online. This group isnГўВҖВҷt necessarily the group that appears when the user logs in to CAS Online. However, if a user regularly uses one account group, the group should be set as both the login group and the active group.
By default, the login account group and active account group show every account (Transit Number) to which the user has access.
To select an account group:
Log in to CAS Online and select
Account Groups
.
To set the login account group:
Select
login
beside the appropriate group. This group will be the default login group. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select Login Account Group
To select the active account group:
Select the name of the appropriate account group under the Account Group column. The accounts in this group will be displayed on the Account Activity and Account Summary pages during the login session. Figure 4 shows an account group that is both the login account group and the active account group.
Note:	Only one account group can be active at a time.
Figure 4: Sample Active and Login Account Group
8.4	Editing an Account Group
To edit an account group:
Log in to CAS Online. Select
Account Groups
,
then select
Edit
beside the appropriate account group. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Edit Account Group
Check or uncheck a box in the Group column to add to or remove an account from the group. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Select Account Group to Edit
Select
Continue
,
then
Confirm
when prompted.
8.5	Deleting an Account Group
To delete an account group:
Log in to CAS Online. Select
Account Groups
, then select
Delete
beside the appropriate account group. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Delete an Account Group
Select
Confirm
when prompted.
Note:	The accounts within the deleted account group arenГўВҖВҷt deleted.
END
